examples.title: Basic ls Commands
examples.description: Common ls command examples for file listing
examples.code:
# Basic listing
ls

# Long format listing
ls -l

# Show all files including hidden
ls -a

# Long format with all files
ls -la

# Human-readable sizes
ls -lh

# Recursive listing
ls -R

# Sort by modification time
ls -lt

# Sort by size (largest first)
ls -lS

# One file per line
ls -1

# Show inode numbers
ls -li

# Classify files
ls -F

# List only directories
ls -ld */
examples.explanation: These are the most commonly used ls command variations in Unix/Linux systems for various file listing purposes.

examples.title: ls Command with Multiple Options
examples.description: Advanced ls command with combined options
examples.code:
# Detailed listing with human-readable sizes
ls -lh

# All files (including hidden) in long format
ls -la

# All files with human-readable sizes
ls -lah

# Reverse time sort (oldest first)
ls -ltr

# Largest files first with sizes
ls -lSh

# Directory details (not contents)
ls -ld dirname

# Numeric UID/GID instead of names
ls -ln

# Long format with color (if supported)
sudo ls --color=auto -l
examples.explanation: Combining multiple options allows for more specific and useful file listings. Flags can be combined in any order.
